title: rubus furvus var. iseranus
intro: Rubus furvus var. iseranus, also known as the black raspberry, is a plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its dark purple to almost black berries. The medicinal properties of Rubus furvus var. iseranus include its high content of antioxidants, anthocyanins, and ellagic acid, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce the risk of heart disease, certain types of cancer, and neurodegenerative diseases, as well as its ability to support digestive health and boost the immune system. Rubus furvus var. iseranus can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and jams, and is also often consumed raw or frozen to reap its nutritional benefits.
